fields.description:
## What
Adopt the B1 batch primitive (`_store.batch_stage_and_commit`, ticket
890d-4733-1c88-4976 — **hard prerequisite; this ticket is `depends_on` B1**) on the NDJSON
importer's independent passes so a bulk import commits `ceil(events/256)` commits instead
of one-per-event.

## Why
`src/rebar/_io/import_ndjson.py` already has idempotency-by-`source_id` and deferred-push,
but each pass still funnels through a per-event commit. The write funnels differ by pass and
several carry STATE-READING GUARDS that must still run — so this is NOT a blanket
"create_core/append_event variant":
- Pass 1 (CREATE) → `rebar.create_ticket` → `composer.create_core` → `_seam.append_event`.
- Pass 2a (parents) → `edit_ticket(parent=…)` → composer/leaf edit path.
- Pass 2b (links) → `rebar.graph._links.add_dependency` → `_write_link_event` →
  `_seam.append_event`. This pass is NOT batchable: `add_dependency` does read-after-write
  within the pass — it issues TWO `_write_link_event` calls for a reciprocal `relates_to`
  and calls `_is_active_link` (an idempotency check that READS committed link state) to
  decide whether to emit the reciprocal. Buffering would defer those writes so a later
  link in the same chunk cannot observe an earlier one (a TOCTOU), so **Pass 2b stays
  per-event** (like Pass 2e).
- Pass 2c (file-impact / verify-commands) and Pass 2d (comments) → `src/rebar/_commands/
  leaf.py` (`set_file_impact` / `set_verify_commands` / comment), NOT `create_core`.

## Scope
- Introduce the compose/commit split at the **`_seam.append_event` layer** (the common
  funnel): each command still runs its own state-reading guards and COMPOSES its event, but
  instead of committing immediately it can RETURN the composed `(ticket_id, event_dict)` to
  a caller-supplied buffer. Guards are preserved; only the final `git commit` is deferred.
- In `src/rebar/_io/import_ndjson.py`, buffer composed events for the **independent,
  append-only passes — Pass 1 (CREATE), Pass 2a (parents), Pass 2c (file-impact/
  verify-commands), Pass 2d (comments)** — and flush each buffer through
  `batch_stage_and_commit` in chunks (default 256, hard-coded — no new config key). These
  passes have no read-after-write within a chunk (each event targets a distinct ticket/
  field and does not read another buffered event's effect).
- Pass 2b (links) and Pass 2e (statuses) stay **per-event**: 2b because of the reciprocal-
  link/`_is_active_link` read-after-write above; 2e because it is STATEFUL (children-
  before-parents, `cascade=False`).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Compose/commit split at `_seam.append_event` (and the leaf/graph write funnels it fronts): a command can return its composed `(ticket_id, event_dict)` without committing, with all existing state-reading guards (link cycle/promotion/idempotency, edit validation) still run; the default committing path is unchanged for every non-importer caller
- [ ] Importer Passes 1, 2a, 2c, 2d buffer composed events and flush via `batch_stage_and_commit` in 256-event chunks; Pass 2b (links) and Pass 2e (statuses) remain per-event
- [ ] A real import of a synthetic N-event NDJSON file (`import_tickets`) that contains **no status transitions** (so Pass 2e contributes zero commits) produces a real tickets branch whose NEW commit count (delta before/after via `git -C <tracker> rev-list --count HEAD`) equals `sum_over_independent_passes(ceil(pass_events/256))` — NOT `ceil(N/256)`, because each independent pass (1, 2a–2d) flushes its own buffer — and is >=10x fewer than the ~2N per-event commits; the import replays to state byte-identical to the per-event path. (If a synthetic file DOES include status transitions, add their per-event Pass-2e commit count to the expected total, since Pass 2e stays one-commit-per-event.)
- [ ] Idempotent re-import (source_id re-scan) still skips already-imported events (no duplicate events, no extra commits)
- [ ] `make lint && make typecheck && python -m pytest tests/ -k import -q` all green
## Deferred-commit opt-in (mechanism)
The compose/commit split is OPT-IN via a new optional keyword arg on the `_seam.append_event`
funnel (and the leaf/graph write helpers that front it): `buffer: list[tuple[str, dict]] |
None = None`. When `buffer is None` (the default) behavior is EXACTLY as today — the event is
composed AND committed — so **every existing caller is unchanged with no need to enumerate
them**. When a caller passes a `buffer`, the composed `(ticket_id, event_dict)` is appended to
it and the immediate commit is skipped; the importer owns the buffer and flushes it via
`batch_stage_and_commit`. State-reading guards (link cycle/promotion/idempotency, edit
validation) run BEFORE the append in both modes.

## Commit-count math (per-pass flush)
The importer flushes each independent pass's buffer separately, so total NEW commits =
`sum_over_passes( ceil(pass_events / 256) )`, NOT `ceil(total/256)`. For a synthetic import of
N=3000 tickets with 1 CREATE + 1 comment each (~3000 CREATE in pass 1, ~3000 COMMENT in pass
2d), that is `ceil(3000/256) + ceil(3000/256) = 12 + 12 = 24` batch commits vs ~6000 per-event
commits (>=10x fewer). The benchmark asserts the **delta** (commits after import − commits
before import), since the tracker carries baseline commits from `init_repo`.
